title: Enoch Walked with God
summary: “And Enoch walked with God after he begat Methuselah three hundred years, and begat sons and daughters: And all the days of Enoch were three hundred sixty and five years: And Enoch walked with God: and he was not; for God took him.” Genesis 5:22-24 God will only walk with a person in whom he takes pleasure. “Can two walk together, except they be agreed?” (Amos 3:3). There is no greater blessing on earth than to be called the friend of God. Friendship is mutually beneficial – both parties derive great pleasure from talking together, sharing activities and just being in each other’s company. What a privilege it would have been to observe this relationship between Enoch and God! What intimacy, what love, what respect and every good blessing flowed to Enoch through this relationship with God! We should all desire a closer walk with Jesus! “But without faith it is impossible to please him: for he that cometh to God must believe that he is, and that he is a rewarder of them that diligently seek him” (Hebrews 11:6). Enoch believed passionately in this scripture. He demonstrated this for 365 years. Imagine that!
